text:
Every evening I fly out to my old orchard. The evenings are cooling now, just shy of winter.

There’s this funny old tree I like that. It’s twisted, gnarled with ivy. They called it the weeping pear. Just the silliest thing. You look at it you think it’s going to fall over at any second. But it just stays there. Every spring, oh my. It blooms, a glorious circle of white flowers. Not like anything else. Just these tiny stars.

You could hear birds singing sweet songs about it. Said they’d sing just to hear themselves disappear into the white blooms. Of course, just birds. I know nothing.

One day I’m walking there, just me thinking how nice it sounds. There’s this little flicker. I’m not noticing him. But then I am. He lands on the branch. Just sits there. Just hums. And hums. This long, long note that goes nowhere. Nothing
